steps: The graph shows \(y = f(x)\) where \(x\) = points earned by Player 1 and \(y\) = points earned by Player 2. The point (49, 0) means \(x = 49\) and \(y = 0\).
Since there are 49 total questions and every correct answer goes to Player 1, Player 1 earning 49 points means Player 1 answered all 49 correctly. Player 2 therefore earns 0 points.

• Choice A — 'When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.': The y-coordinate is 0, not 49. ✗
  • Choice B — 'When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.': Correctly reads x=49 as Player 1’s score and y=0 as Player 2’s score. ✓
  • Choice C — 'When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.': Reverses the coordinates; the x-value is 49, not 0. ✗
  • Choice D — 'When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.': Both values are wrong; this would describe the point (0, 0), not (49, 0). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
